etymology.description: semantic 火(火) (huǒ, "fire") + phonetic 盧(盧) (lú, "surname Lu") -> 熱 source, heating stone -> oven, stove
etymology.interpretation: The fire 火(火) (huǒ, "fire") component indicates that the character relates to a heated source or combustion, while the phonetic 盧(盧) (lú, "surname Lu") provides the pronunciation. Together, these elements refer to a structure for containing and managing fire, such as an oven or stove.
etymology.simplification: Reduced to fewer strokes by maintaining the 火(火, "fire") component in the lower section while simplifying the 盧(盧) (lú, "surname Lu") portion for a streamlined form.

examples.chinese: 他在厨房里有一个新炉子(他在廚房裡有一個新爐子)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài chúfáng lǐ yǒu yī gè xīn lúzi.
examples.english: He has a new oven in the kitchen.

examples.chinese: 我们用炉子烤面包(我們用爐子烤麵包)。
examples.english: We use the oven to bake bread.

examples.chinese: 炉子打坏了(爐子打壞了)，需要修理(需要修理)。
examples.pinyin: Lúzi dǎ huài le, xūyào xiūlǐ.
examples.english: The oven is broken and needs repair.
